operation: First, initialize a workplane on the XY plane, draw a circle with radius 17.75 units, and extrude the circle 6 units along the positive Z-axis to form a cylindrical section. Next, create a new workplane on the lower (<Z) face of the cylinder, draw a regular 6-sided polygon with a diameter of 45.5 units, and extrude this polygon 4 units along the negative Z-axis to form a hexagonal base. Finally, create a workplane on the upper (>Z) face of the cylinder, generate a for-construction regular 22-sided polygon with 30.5 unit diameter, then cut 3.4 unit diameter through holes at every vertex of the construction polygon, extending fully through the cylindrical section.
geometry: The model is a coaxial stacked structure, with a flat regular hexagonal prism as the lower base, and a right circular cylinder of smaller width centered on the top face of the hexagon. The upper cylinder has 22 identical, evenly spaced through holes arranged in a concentric circular ring around its central axis, running the full height of the cylinder.
function: This is a 22-tooth GT-series timing pulley with an integrated anti-rotation hexagonal mounting base, designed for precision rotational motion transmission in 3D printers and motion control systems, with a top bolt hole pattern for easy assembly.
